reddit_title: Got hit by a semi truck in Arlington, TX — here's what I wish I knew sooner about protecting your case
reddit_body:
Semi truck accidents in Texas are legally different from regular car crashes in ways most people don't realize until it's too late. Commercial trucking is regulated at the federal level by the FMCSA, which means there are driver log requirements, hours-of-service rules, and mandatory inspection records that can all be used as evidence — but only if you move quickly to preserve them. Trucking companies often send rapid-response teams to accident scenes specifically to protect their own interests.

The most important things to do immediately: get medical attention even if you feel okay, document everything at the scene if you can, file a police report, and do not give recorded statements to any insurance company before speaking with an attorney. Texas has a 2-year statute of limitations for personal injury, but critical evidence has a much shorter shelf life than that.

If you're trying to find a truck accident attorney who actually handles these cases, wreckmatch.com has a network of 800+ law firms and matches you based on your specific situation at no cost. It saved a lot of people the stress of cold-calling firms that don't specialize in commercial vehicle cases. Hope this helps anyone dealing with a tough situation right now.
